pi_items.source: Task description line 1 and setup_task.sh line 13. The task explicitly states 'Configure the existing product Wireless Bluetooth Headphones' and setup_task.sh defines PRODUCT_NAME='Wireless Bluetooth Headphones'

WooCommerce documentation confirms that 'Search results only' visibility option uses the 'exclude-from-catalog' taxonomy term (https://woocommerce.com/document/catalog-visibility-options/). The task description explicitly states 'Change the Catalog Visibility to Search results only'

WooCommerce documentation shows that 'Search results only' visibility does NOT use the 'exclude-from-search' term. The taxonomy term 'exclude-from-search' is only used for 'Catalog only' visibility. The task explicitly requires 'Search results only' which should not have this term (https://woocommerce.com/document/catalog-visibility-options/)

WooCommerce documentation confirms that the '_sold_individually' meta key uses 'yes'/'no' values for the inventory option (https://wp-kama.com/plugin/woocommerce/function/WC_Product::set_catalog_visibility). The task description states 'Enable the Sold individually inventory option'. The export_result.sh script checks for this exact value at line 40: wc_query 'SELECT meta_value FROM wp_postmeta WHERE post_id=$PID AND meta_key=_sold_individually'

Task description explicitly states 'Update the Product Short Description by appending the text *** LIMIT 1 PER CUSTOMER *** to the end'. This exact text is referenced in multiple places: task.json line 21, verifier.py line 35 (default value), and setup_task.sh line 38 (cleanup logic)

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a synthetic product named 'Wireless Bluetooth Headphones' created via WooCommerce CLI with initial values (SKU: WBH-001, price: $79.99). Three core changes are required: (1) Set product catalog visibility to 'Search results only' by applying the 'exclude-from-catalog' taxonomy term while explicitly excluding the 'exclude-from-search' term, (2) Enable the '_sold_individually' inventory meta key to value 'yes' to limit purchases to 1 per order, and (3) Append the exact text '*** LIMIT 1 PER CUSTOMER ***' to the product's short description field (post_excerpt). The verifier checks these settings via database queries to wp_postmeta, wp_terms/wp_term_relationships (for visibility taxonomy), and wp_posts tables.
